data.title: Shake Shack GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.03, revenue of $356.5M beats by $2.37M
data.description:
• Shake Shack press release (NYSE:SHAK): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.03.
  • Revenue of $356.5M (+12.6% Y/Y) beats by $2.37M.
  • System-wide sales of $549.9 million, up 13.7% versus 2024.
  • Same-Shack sales up 1.8% versus 2024.
  • Operating income of $22.4 million versus operating income of $10.8 million in 2024.
    • Restaurant-level profit(1) of $82.2 million, or 23.9% of Shack sales.
  • Net income of $18.5 million versus net income of $10.4 million in 2024.
    • Adjusted EBITDA(1) of $58.9 million, up 24.8% versus 2024.

data.title: Vulcan Materials misses Q2 estimates
data.description:
• Vulcan Materials press release (NYSE:VMC): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.45 misses by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $2.1B (+4.5% Y/Y) misses by $100M.
  • Regarding the Company's outlook, Mr. Hill said, "Our execution in the first half of the year along with an acceleration in new highway construction activity in our markets supports our full-year outlook to deliver $2.35 to $2.55 billion of Adjusted EBITDA.  As always, we will remain focused on factors within our control, including pricing and operating disciplines that drive earnings growth and cash generation."

      • Excludes items outside of our core business operations

data.title: Udemy plans to grow consumer subscriptions to over 250,000 by year-end as AI initiatives accelerate
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Udemy (UDMY) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Hugo Sarrazin highlighted that "Udemy reported a strong quarter as we exceeded expectations for revenue and adjusted EBITDA." Sarrazin emphasized the company's operational improvements and strategic focus, stating, "This firmly positions us for growth acceleration in the coming quarters."
  • Sarrazin provided updates on strategic priorities: increasing emphasis on subscription products, expanding the partnership ecosystem, executing global market activation, and positioning Udemy as the leading AI-powered skill acceleration platform. "With approximately 70% of our top line now subscription-based, we are building a more predictable revenue stream," Sarrazin noted. He announced a new milestone of 200,000 paid consumer subscribers and projected growth to "more than 250,000 by year-end."
  • Udemy will pilot programmatic advertising on free courses to unlock new revenue streams and strengthen upsell to paid subscriptions. Strategic partnerships with Indeed, UKG, and BCN Global were highlighted to broaden reach and product integration.
  • Sarrazin shared that Udemy is launching a beta test of the model context protocol server with enterprise customers, aiming to embed personalized learning within AI-powered applications. He stated, "Since announcing our beta program last week, we have received overwhelming response from enterprise customers that want to be included."
  • On product innovation, Sarrazin introduced the new CTO, Ozzie Goldschmied, to accelerate AI platform development and shared success with AI-powered roleplay and in-course AI assistant products. Sarrazin said, "We have more than 7,000 unique role play creation by Instructor. That's more than the entire category combined."
  • CFO Sarah Walter Blanchard stated, "We achieved a significant milestone in Udemy's financial journey with our first quarter of positive GAAP net income since our IPO." Blanchard highlighted strong revenue of $200 million, Udemy Business ARR of $520 million, and gross margin improvement. She also noted, "Adjusted EBITDA was approximately $28 million or 14% of revenue, representing an 1,100 basis point year-over-year expansion."

Outlook

  • Blanchard provided Q3 guidance: "We expect revenue to be between $190 million and $195 million... Udemy Business revenue increases approximately 3% year-over-year, while consumer revenue is expected to be down 9%. On the bottom line, we expect to deliver adjusted EBITDA of $18 million to $20 million or approximately 10% of revenue."
  • Full year 2025 revenue is expected in the range of $784 million to $794 million. Blanchard stated, "This implies Udemy Business revenue increases approximately 5% year-over-year and consumer revenue will be down approximately 8% year-over-year at the midpoint of the range."
  • Adjusted EBITDA guidance for the year was raised to $84 million to $89 million.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q2 was $200 million, up 3% year-over-year, with Udemy Business segment delivering $129 million and ARR of $520 million.
  • Gross margin improved to 67%, and operating expenses were $112 million or 56% of revenue.
  • Blanchard stated, "We delivered positive GAAP net income of approximately $6 million, a remarkable improvement from a loss of $32 million in Q2 of last year."
  • Adjusted EBITDA reached $28 million, marking the 14th consecutive quarter of exceeding expectations on the bottom line. Free cash flow was $39 million, and cash and marketable securities totaled $393 million at quarter-end.

Q&A

  • Joshua Phillip Baer, Morgan Stanley, asked Sarrazin about key observations in his first four months. Sarrazin replied, "AI reskilling is the topic in every geography... They want more than an online catalog. They're wanting more ROI, and they want more AI."
  • Baer inquired about COVID contracts and net dollar retention. Blanchard responded, "When these come up for renewal, without those things in place, we may have a downsell, we may have some logo churn."
  • Ryan Michael MacDonald, Needham & Company, asked about renewal headwinds and pipeline optimism. Blanchard explained, "We do have some headwinds from a renewal perspective. But what we're seeing is a lot of momentum on the pipeline build... all of these leading indicators from a bookings perspective give us great visibility into a substantial increase in net new ARR in the third quarter."
  • MacDonald asked about programmatic advertising. Sarrazin shared, "We're going to deploy new ways to monetize our assets. And ad is one example... what we're doing in Q3 is we're doing some experiments to understand how it helps in certain use case and with certain audience."
  • Jason Ross Tilchen, Canaccord Genuity, inquired about AI packages. Sarrazin noted, "The reception has been overwhelmingly positive... by merchandising more precisely around AI and AI fluency, we're also making it easier for our sales team to come with a very targeted value proposition."
  • Other analysts probed technology differentiation, consumer strategy, win rates, and sales and marketing leverage, with management emphasizing product innovation, upmarket focus, and operational efficiency.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on renewal headwinds, consumer traction, and AI monetization, displaying a neutral to slightly positive tone with a focus on execution and pipeline visibility.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, with Sarrazin stating, "I couldn't be more excited about how we're pivoting to be relevant in a LLM-centric technology stack," and Blanchard emphasizing, "We are confident that we can deliver sustainable, profitable growth."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more assertive about inflection points and operational improvements, while analysts continued to seek clarity on growth drivers and headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Management's strategic focus shifted from initial vision setting to tangible execution on subscription growth, new revenue streams, and AI platform leadership.
  • Consumer subscription revenue as a percentage increased, with 200,000 paid subscribers reached compared to the prior focus on a 40% year-over-year increase in Q1.
  • Gross margin and adjusted EBITDA expanded further, and the company delivered its first positive GAAP net income since IPO.
  • Analyst questions in both quarters centered on renewal rates, customer expansion, and AI product differentiation, but the current quarter featured deeper inquiry into monetization opportunities and operational leverage.
  • Management's confidence level increased, supported by concrete financial milestones and early product traction.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited renewal headwinds from legacy COVID-era contracts, noting these will continue to impact net dollar retention in the near term.
  • Churn and lower expansion rates in the SMB cohort were mentioned as partial offsets to growth.
  • Macro uncertainty and headwinds in certain regions and customer segments remain a factor, though management is optimistic about pipeline build and upmarket focus.
  • Analyst concerns focused on the timing and sustainability of green shoots, potential cannibalization from subscription migration, and the ramp-up of new revenue streams.

Final Takeaway

Udemy's management highlighted a quarter of operational and financial milestones, including reaching 200,000 paid consumer subscribers, delivering positive GAAP net income, and launching AI-driven product innovations. Strategic priorities around subscription expansion, new partnerships, and AI platform leadership are gaining traction, with management projecting continued momentum in the second half of 2025. The company is increasingly confident in reaching an inflection point for growth as it leverages strong pipeline development, product differentiation, and a disciplined approach to cost management and innovation.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: KKR & Co. GAAP EPS of $0.50, revenue of $5.09B beats by $3.25B
data.description:
• KKR & Co. press release (NYSE:KKR): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.50.
  • Revenue of $5.09B (+22.1% Y/Y) beats by $3.25B.
  • FeeRelatedEarnings(“FRE”)of $887 million ($0.98/adj. share) in the quarter, up 17% yearover-year.
  • FRE was $3.6 billion in the LTM ($3.96/adj. share), up 34% year-over-year.
  • TotalOperating Earnings ("TOE") of $1.2 billion ($1.33/adj. share) in the quarter, up 14% year-over-year.
  • TOE was $4.7 billion in the LTM ($5.19/adj. share), up 26% year-over-year.
  • AdjustedNetIncome (“ANI”)of $1.1 billion ($1.18/adj. share) in the quarter, up 9% yearover-year.
  • ANI was $4.5 billion in the LTM ($4.98/adj. share), up 27% year-over-year.
  • AssetsUnderManagement(“AUM”)of $686 billion, up 14% year-over-year.

data.title: Utz Brands Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 misses by $0.01, revenue of $366.7M beats by $4.73M
data.description:
• Utz Brands press release (NYSE:UTZ): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $366.7M (+2.9% Y/Y) beats by $4.73M.
  • Total Organic Net Sales increased 2.9%; Branded Salty Snacks increased 5.4%

  • Fiscal Year 2025 Outlook

  • The Company is updating its 2025 fiscal year outlook to reflect stronger top-line trends and higher Adjusted EBITDA. The company is lowering expected Adjusted EPS growth due to higher capital expenditures, depreciation and amortization, and interest expense.
  • Organic Net Sales growth of 2.5% or better, compared to the prior expectation of low-single digits.
  • Adjusted Earnings Per Share growth of 7% to 10%, compared to the prior expectation of 10% to 15%

data.title: Bombardier reports mixed Q2 results; on track to meet full-year guidance
data.description:
• Bombardier press release (OTCQX:BDRBF): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.11 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $2B (-9.1% Y/Y) misses by $120M.
  • Corporation delivered 36 aircraft for the quarter.
  • Adjusted EBITDA(1) totaled $297 million and reported EBIT was $205 million for the quarter, down 11% and up 7% year-over-year respectively.
  • Free cash flow usage came in at $164 million, compared to $68 million free cash flow usage for the same quarter of 2024.
  • Unit book-to-bill(5) reached 2.3; backlog(6) jumped to $16.1 billion as at June 30, 2025. This marks Bombardier’s highest single-quarter business jet unit order volume in more than a decade.
  • Available liquidity(1) at $1.2 billion; cash and cash equivalents were $811 million as at June 30, 2025.
  • The company's Services business maintained impressive revenue momentum reporting 16% growth year-over-year and reaching $590 million for the quarter. The company continues to recruit technicians worldwide to maximize its footprint, while adding conveniently located services for customers.
  • The company is on track to meet full-year guidance.
